rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical bromination of limonene (olefin) via anodic oxidation of bromide to bromine, which adds across the alkene
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bromide to bromine (Br2), which then reacts with the olefin; tribromide (Br3-) serves as a reservoir in equilibrium with Br2
electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Br- to Br2 (E ~0.49 V); Br2 either equilibrates with excess Br- to form Br3- (resting state) or is consumed by the olefin via electrophilic addition. In the presence of substrate the Br3- oxidation wave diminishes and no reduction wave is observed, indicating Br2 is trapped by the alkene.

probe_reagents.name: NaBr (bromide source) CV
probe_reagents.observation: Quasi-reversible oxidation at 0.49 V (Br-/Br2) and second oxidation at 0.86 V (Br3-/Br2); reduction wave at -0.21 V

probe_reagents.name: (S)-(-)-limonene with NaBr CV
probe_reagents.observation: First oxidation shifts from 0.49 V to 0.58 V, second oxidation wave reduced, and reduction wave absent — consistent with electrogenerated Br2 being consumed by the olefin

notes: Mechanism supported solely by CV experiments comparing NaBr alone vs NaBr + limonene; Br3-/Br2 equilibrium acts as a bromine reservoir. No radical traps, KIE, or DFT reported on this page.
